concerns.text: SC-003 (robustness measured against baseline model stability R² > 0.8) lacks a dedicated task to measure and report the stability metric (e.g., count of successful windows or average R²). T012 only handles skipping invalid windows, not aggregating the success rate required by the success criterion.

FR-007 (Null Model Baseline via window shuffling) is split into T020a-c but lacks a task to aggregate the shuffled results into a comparable null distribution (e.g., calculating mean rho of shuffled runs) required to satisfy SC-004 and the spec's definition of a baseline.

FR-004b (flagging 'high drift' if p-value < 0.05) is assigned to T018, but T018 depends on T023 (block permutation) and T020c (null comparison). T020c is in Phase 4 while T023 is in Phase 5, creating a broken dependency where the flagging logic requires a null baseline comparison that isn't fully integrated in the metrics pipeline yet.

T015 (variance check) implies a one-time setup in preprocess.py, but the spec's Edge Cases require a per-window operation ('drop that feature for that specific window'). No task explicitly places the variance check inside the window iteration loop (T014) to ensure per-window rank integrity.

T026a/b (global stats aggregation) are assigned to US3, but SC-003 (robustness measurement) applies to the entire project. Placing aggregation solely in US3 risks decoupling the robustness metric from US1/US2 outputs if US3 is deferred, violating the Single Source of Truth principle for project-wide stats.

T020a-c (Null Model Baseline) follow the spec's window-shuffling approach, but the plan.md 'Statistical Methodology Update' describes a 'Magnitude Test' that permutes feature importance vectors. The tasks do not implement or explain the plan's vector-permutation approach, leaving a methodological gap between plan and execution.
